sourceMovie.title: Disobedience
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Disobedience works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Brokeback Mountain
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama energy of Disobedience is alive in Brokeback Mountain (2005), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a bittersweet payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Romeo + Juliet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Romeo + Juliet (1996) operates in the same drama as Disobedience, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The bittersweet payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Best of Me
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Best of Me (2014) belongs to the same conversation as Disobedience — both anchor their drama in romance emotion that doesn't let go. The bittersweet undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Endless Love
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Disobedience's drama stayed with you, Endless Love (2014) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The romance through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: My Policeman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Disobedience excels in drama, My Policeman (2022) doubles down on romance to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Love at First Sight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Love at First Sight (2023) is the natural next step after Disobedience: same drama DNA, different perspective. The bittersweet streak and romance elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Maurice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Disobedience who respond to its drama will find Maurice (1987) immediately familiar. Both films share a romance sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Duke of Burgundy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Disobedience fans keep landing on The Duke of Burgundy (2015) — the drama is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Once Upon a Time in America
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Once Upon a Time in America draws from the same drama well as Disobedience, channeling crime in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1984 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Seven Samurai
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Seven Samurai (1954) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Disobedience later expanded. If Disobedience resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

faq.question: What should I watch after Disobedience?
faq.answer: Try Brokeback Mountain, Romeo + Juliet, The Best of Me—each shares DNA with Disobedience while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Disobedience?
faq.answer: Disobedience (2018) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Disobedience worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
